mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal income and a decline in the consumer price index expands real purchasing power, boosting consumer discretionary spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against a univariate baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets higher borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, yielding negative returns in more than 65% of instances, or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong construction sector demand and positive wealth effects for households, which systematically lifts equity market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or housing starts show no correlation with equity returns after adjusting for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 320.0, it reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.0, or the threshold loses predictive validity after 2023 data revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals economic contraction that rapidly erodes corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP declines fail to predict negative equity returns in more than 60% of late-cycle instances, or the signal is subsumed by monetary policy variables in multivariate regression.
